what_it_does: Defines window.CV_AI, the Universal AI Registry — one hierarchical registry of parametric, inheritable entries across five layers (provider, behaviour, skill, capability, context) with an API that mirrors CV_REGISTRY (register/registerMany/update/remove/get/all/query/resolve/lineage/children/subscribe). It implements provider runtime resolution (claude/openai-image/CV_HOST-delegated), surface-keyed context resolution, behaviour composition, and execute() — the generative path ai-output = f(capability, context, params) — with loud failures throughout. Built-ins live in memory; user/vi entries persist to localStorage.
observations: LAYERS = provider/behaviour/skill/capability/context, with LAYER_INFO carrying label, rank, swatch color, and desc per layer.
Two stores: BUILTIN (in-memory, re-seeded each load) and USER (localStorage-persisted under 'cv:ai-registry:user-entries'); register routes by provenance.
register throws on missing id or unknown layer; update forks a built-in into user space on edit (mirrors CV_REGISTRY contract), bumping version.
query supports layer/layers/family/families/surface/tags/provenance/extendsId/search and sorts by layer rank then name; surface filter treats '*' or absent surfaces as match-any.
resolve() flattens an entry with its ancestors (leaf wins), merging params/behaviours/surfaces/tags and carrying functional members; lineage walks the extends chain with cycle guard.

resolveProvider binds a provider to its live runtime: kind 'claude' needs window.claude.complete, 'openai-image' needs window.cvOpenAI; any other kind is delegated to window.CV_HOST.resolveProviderRuntime, else throws — no silent fallback.
resolveContext picks the most specific surface context resolver (falling back to context.generic) and merges its resolveCtx output; throws if a resolver fails.

execute() resolves the capability (+inheritance); if it's a skill it delegates to its target capability threading the instruction as brief; supports both a run() path (capability owns build→complete→parse) and a build/parse path; every failure throws with a descriptive message listing registered ids.
ACTIVE object records 'what screen Vi is on' via setActiveSurface; CV_AI.complete is a convenience that routes a one-off completion through the resolved claude provider.
normalize() fills defaults and carries functional members through untouched (run/build/parse/resolveCtx/text/instruction/target/runtime/modality/caps).
standouts: Deliberately mirrors CV_REGISTRY's API verbatim so the two registries are learnable as one — same register/resolve/lineage/query/subscribe shape.
execute() is the single generative path expressing ai-output = f(capability, context, params), unifying skills, run-capabilities, and build/parse-capabilities through one dispatcher.
Provider resolution is loud and layered: known kinds bound directly, unknown kinds delegated to CV_HOST, else throw with activation guidance — no degrade-to-default anywhere.
Built-in-forks-on-edit contract: editing a built-in creates a versioned user copy with forkedFrom set, preserving the original.
conventions: Loud-fail discipline: throws (with the list of registered ids) on missing/wrong-layer entries, missing runtimes, failed resolvers — matches the project's non-negotiables.
Registry-as-single-source: the running interface is meant to be a projection of this catalogue (register data, not write code).
API parity with CV_REGISTRY and the same layer/inheritance idea (single-inheritance via extends).
Catch blocks that swallow are limited to localStorage I/O and listener notify — not to the generative/resolution path.
concerns: loadFromStorage and saveUser swallow all exceptions silently (empty catch) — localStorage failures pass unnoticed; acceptable for persistence but a quiet spot in an otherwise loud system.
notify() swallows listener exceptions silently (try{}catch{}), so a broken subscriber fails quietly.
notes: This is the heart of the AI layer: one CV_REGISTRY-shaped registry of provider/behaviour/skill/capability/context entries with a single loud execute() generative path and runtime-binding provider resolution — register data to add an AI move.
questions: resolveProvider's 'claude' branch requires window.claude.complete and openai-image requires window.cvOpenAI — where are those live runtimes injected, and what guarantees they exist before execute() runs?
Skills delegate to a target capability but skills are excluded from the capability query in error messages — is there a query path that surfaces skills to the UI separately?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header describes a unified, CV_REGISTRY-shaped AI registry with a single generative execute path and loud provider/context resolution, and the implementation delivers exactly that.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth / one catalogue for all AI moves
API parity across registries (learnable as one)
ai-output = f(capability, context, params) as the generative discipline
intention_signals: Trying to express the entire AI layer in the same registry shape as the rest of the unified system — so 'what Vi can do, with what, knowing where it is' is one inheritable, queryable, projectable catalogue, and adding an AI move is data not code.
novelty: Treating AI capabilities/providers/behaviours/context as Types in a registry isomorphic to the design-type registry, with one execute() that handles skill delegation, run-capabilities, and build/parse-capabilities uniformly, plus CV_HOST delegation for unknown runtimes.
contribution: Contributes the CV_AI registry engine itself: the layer model, the mirrored register/resolve/lineage/query API, the loud provider runtime resolution (incl. CV_HOST delegation), surface-keyed context resolution, behaviour composition, and the unified execute() generative dispatcher.
summary_for_embedding: window.CV_AI, a CV_REGISTRY-shaped hierarchical AI registry (provider/behaviour/skill/capability/context) with register/resolve/lineage/query/subscribe, loud provider runtime binding, surface-keyed context resolution, and a single execute() generative path.
intention_for_embedding: To express the whole AI layer as one inheritable, queryable registry mirroring the design-type registry, so every AI move is single-sourced data and runs through one loud generative dispatcher.
